title: 104 AI Career Coach
summary: Redesigned 104's career Q&A into a context-adaptive AI coach that reads where users are in their career journey before giving advice.

detail.overview: Designed an AI career coaching feature for 104 (Taiwan's largest job platform, 10M+ users), going through a failed V1 POC and a successful V2 redesign.
detail.problem: Job seekers at career turning points lacked real-time, personalized guidance. The existing Q&A format couldn't read the user's career context.
detail.process: V1 integrated AI into the Q&A format—POC failed because users didn't know what to ask. Went back to user interviews, redefined the problem. V2 shifted to context-adaptive design: the system identifies which of 3 career scenarios the user is in (actively job-hunting, passively considering, career-lost) before surfacing relevant guidance.
detail.outcome: V2 PRD approved internally and entered engineering backlog.
detail.reflection: V1's failure taught me: AI feature design starts not from 'what can the model do' but 'what is the user's mental model in this moment.'

title: Coffee Origin Map
summary: A gacha-mechanic card collection game for a company coffee club—scan QR codes to pull origin cards, built full-stack from PRD to deployment.

detail.overview: End-to-end product build: 70+ page PRD, React 18 frontend, Node.js/Express backend, Supabase database, LINE Login OAuth 2.0, QR code gacha mechanic, and admin JWT dashboard.
detail.problem: The company coffee club's knowledge-sharing was passive and lacked reasons for members to return.
detail.process: Designed a gacha mechanic to create surprise and reward—each pull reveals a coffee origin card with flavor notes and coordinates. Used LINE Login to minimize friction. Iterated PRD alongside development, reprioritizing features based on usability testing.
detail.outcome: Core gacha mechanic and admin dashboard shipped. 3-phase roadmap defined for gamification expansion.
detail.reflection: Building this as both PM and developer made me viscerally understand the cost of scope changes. My PRD writing is tighter now.
